title: The Download: NASA’s nuclear spacecraft and unveiling our AI 10
description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. NASA is building the first nuclear reactor-powered interplanetary spacecraft. How will it work?  Just before Artemis II began its historic slings

title: Cyberscammers are bypassing banks’ security with illicit tools sold on Telegram
description: From inside a money-laundering center in Cambodia, an employee opens a popular Vietnamese banking app on his phone. The app asks him to upload a photo associated with the account, so he clicks on a picture of a 30-something Asian man. Next, the app requests to open the camera for a video “liveness”

title: AI Slop Is Making the Internet Fake-Happy
description: A new study examines the impact of the rise of AI-generated websites on the internet—and found some surprising results.

title: The Deepfake Nudes Crisis in Schools Is Much Worse Than You Thought
description: An analysis by WIRED and Indicator found nearly 90 schools and 600 students around the world impacted by AI-generated deepfake nude images—and the problem shows no signs of going away.

title: No one’s sure if synthetic mirror life will kill us all
description: For four days in February 2019, some 30 synthetic biologists and ethicists hunkered down at a conference center in Northern Virginia to brainstorm high-risk, cutting-­edge, irresistibly exciting ideas that the National Science Foundation should fund. By the end of the meeting, they’d landed on a com

title: Building trust in the AI era with privacy-led UX
description: The practice of privacy-led user experience (UX) is a design philosophy that treats transparency around data collection and usage as an integral part of the customer relationship. An undertapped opportunity in digital marketing, privacy-led UX treats user consent not as a tick-box compliance exercis

title: Anthropic’s rise is giving some OpenAI investors second thoughts
description: One investor who has backed both companies told the FT that justifying OpenAI's recent round required assuming an IPO valuation of $1.2 trillion or more — making Anthropic's current $380 billion valuation look like the relative bargain.

title: Ukraine’s military robot surge aims to offset drone risks to humans
description: Ukraine is replacing more soldiers with robots in the battlefield kill zone.

title: Sony killing features for antenna, set-top box users of Bravia smart TVs in May
description: Some 2023 and 2024 models are also affected.

title: Americans ask AI for health care. Hospitals think the answer is more chatbots.
description: Do you trust AI chatbots for health advice? What about one in your patient portal?

title: Two-year-old Surface PCs get $300 price hikes as sub-$1,000 models go away
description: "Paying more for the same stuff" is the story of consumer technology in 2026.

title: Apple chooses Amazon satellites for iPhone, years after rejecting Starlink offer
description: Amazon announces $11.6B merger with Globalstar and satellite deal with Apple.

title: Anthropic co-founder confirms the company briefed the Trump administration on Mythos
description: In an interview at the Semafor World Economy summit this week, Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark explained why the company was still engaged with the U.S. government while simultaneously suing them.

title: Redefining the future of software engineering
description: Software engineering has experienced two seismic shifts this century. First was the rise of the open source movement, which gradually made code accessible to developers and engineers everywhere. Second, the adoption of development operations (DevOps) and agile methodologies took software from siloed

title: Max Hodak’s Science Corp. is preparing to place its first sensor in a human brain
description: The device could help address multiple neurological conditions if it proves successful. One early use could be delivering gentle electrical stimulation to damaged brain or spinal cord cells to encourage healing.

title: Google adds AI Skills to Chrome to help you save favorite workflows
description: Google is adding “Skills” to Chrome, letting users save and reuse AI prompts across websites. The feature builds on Gemini’s browser integration.

title: How to Use Google Chrome’s New AI-Powered ‘Skills’
description: The premade Skills available through the Gemini sidebar in Chrome include ways to maximize protein in recipes or summarize YouTube videos.

title: In just a couple weeks, StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together
description:
The first StrictlyVC of the year will be coming to San Francisco before you know it.

There are still a few tickets available to join us and our stacked speaker lineup April 30, so register today!

title: Anthropic Opposes the Extreme AI Liability Bill That OpenAI Backed
description: Anthropic and OpenAI are clashing over a proposed Illinois law that would let AI labs largely off the hook for mass deaths and financial disasters.

title: Silicon Valley Is Spending Millions to Stop One of Its Own
description: Alex Bores, a former Palantir employee, helped pass one of the country’s toughest AI laws. Now Silicon Valley’s biggest names are trying to stop his rise to Congress.

title: Understanding Convolutions on Graphs
description: Understanding the building blocks and design choices of graph neural networks.

title: A Gentle Introduction to Graph Neural Networks
description: What components are needed for building learning algorithms that leverage the structure and properties of graphs?

title: Distill Hiatus
description: After five years, Distill will be taking a break.

title: Adversarial Reprogramming of Neural Cellular Automata
description: Reprogramming Neural CA to exhibit novel behaviour, using adversarial attacks.

title: Weight Banding
description: Weights in the final layer of common visual models appear as horizontal bands. We investigate how and why.
